australian_relation: Official Adelaide Gaol public page with source-stated William Baker Ashton apparition/spirit account at a verified South Australian historic site.
coordinate_evidence_note: Official Adelaide Gaol page names Adelaide Gaol as the ghost-sighting setting; coordinates use the known Adelaide Gaol public historic site location in South Australia.
evidence_summary: Governor William Baker Ashton. William Baker Ashton was the first Governor of the Adelaide Gaol and despite being a reasonably fair man, he was accused of wrong-doing. The ensuing scandal is said to have hastened his demise. William was a very large man and when he died in office in 1854, his body could not be manipulated down his apartment's steep, narrow staircase. Instead, he was unceremoniously lowered out of the front window to the undertakers waiting below. Three months after his death, William was exonerated. Too little, too late to pacify a disturbed spirit. On warm, still nights with a hint of thunder in the air, his.
publication_or_organisation: Adelaide Gaol Ghostly sightings; Department for Environment and Water, South Australia
title: William Baker Ashton in Ghostly sightings
